new_classes.label_de: Qualität der Aromatisierung mit Pflanzen oder anderen Stoffen
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a wine indicating that it has been flavoured with plants or aromatic substances (including egg yolks, almonds, and similar natural additives), as required for classification under CN heading 2205.

new_classes.label_en: Vermouth and other wine of fresh grapes, flavoured with plants or aromatic substances (CN 2205)
new_classes.label_de: Wermutwein und andere Weine aus frischen Weintrauben, mit Pflanzen oder anderen Stoffen aromatisiert (KN 2205)
new_classes.definition_en: A beverage that is a wine of fresh grapes (or based on such wine) and has been flavoured with plants or aromatic substances, including products such as Marsala all'uovo, Marsala alla mandorla, and Crema di Marsala all'uovo. Classified under CN heading 2205.

coverage_explanation: The two primary discriminating criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) the wine must be of fresh grapes (modelled via the existing FreshGrapeOriginQuality), and (2) it must be flavoured with plants or aromatic substances (modelled via the new AromaticSubstanceFlavouring quality). The specific exemplary products mentioned (Marsala all'uovo, Marsala alla mandorla, Crema di Marsala all'uovo) are illustrative instances referenced in the HS Explanatory Note and are not further sub-classified here — they are documented in the definition text. A minor gap exists in that the full scope of 'aromatic substances' (which in the HS note may include minerals and other non-plant substances) is modelled as a single quality without internal substructure, and the distinction from heading 2204 (unflavoured wine of fresh grapes) is implied by the flavouring restriction but not expressed as an explicit disjointness axiom with WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204 due to the risk of inconsistency if both flavouring qualities could co-occur. Coverage is therefore set to 0.85.
